calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Aug-17-2006 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: Judge Pro Tem Elliot Myles, clerk-Beth Wilber, nothing on record:
8/16/06, 9:10am
Cause called for Small Claims Appeal. Berta Tevalan, Respondent, appears in Pro Per; Natalia Collins, appellant appears in Pro Per. (James Mark Millar is no longer representing Collins Natalia.) Ms. Tevalan appears with an interpreter, Naomi Adelso. Ms. Collins appears with an interpreter, Douglas Haape.
Parties, previously sworn, proceed to present their cases in the narrative, with help from their respective interpreters. Judge Myles questions parties.
REXH1 Dept. of Building Inspection Violation Report
REXH2 9/27/06 letter
REXH3 8/27/05 letter from Natalie Collins
10:00am
Court adjourns, matter is submitted. Evidence is returned to parties

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jul-12-2006 Continued To Aug-16-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 525
MINI MINUTES: Judge Pro Tem Bruce M. Lubarsky, clerk-Beth Wilber, nothing on record:
Cause called for small claims appeal. Appellant, Natalie Collins, appears through James Millar, Esq.; Respondent, Berta Tevalan appears in Pro Per. Parties and witnesses were previously sworn. Mr. Millar lodges a trial brief with the Court, which is placed in the file. Appearing with Ms. Tevalon is Ramone Orellana
9:52am
Judge Pro Tem Lubarsky requests that it be put on the record that the respondent hardly speaks any English. The appellant, Ms. Collins, does not speak English well either, but Mr. Millar, her attorney, speaks fluent Spanish. Mr. Millar told the Court that if all parties would stipulate, he would be willing to Interpret Spanish to English, English to Spanish, for all parties. Judge Lubarsky asks Mr. Millar to ask the Respondents if they are agreeable. Respondents do not agree to having Mr. Millar interpret for them.
10:05am
Court takes a brief break while Mr. Millar checks his schedule for a new date. Parties understand that they will have a new Judge Pro Tem and will have to sign a new Stipulation and Order.
10:10am
Court takes brief recess. Mr. Millar is allowed to review the evidence in the file before the matter is continued.
10:23am
Judge Lubarsky asks that the record note - and Mr. Millar interpret for all parties - a statement re not having a Spanish Interpreter for Respondents. The record should reflect that Mr. Millar was not in favor of a continuance. The Court, however, felt in the interests of justice that the matter should be continued conditional upon:
1. No further continuances
2. Each party must provide their own interpreter
3. Each party agrees that a different Pro Tem can hear the case
Respondents are ordered to appear with a Spanish Interpreter
